scenario: Wren Insurance models a Camunda 8.8 claims process and writes a Camunda Spring Boot Starter worker that handles 4100 jobs per day. The developer wants the handler signature to materialize process variables as a strongly typed MyProcessVariables object so the worker can mutate fields and return the object to push changes back to Zeebe, and a reviewer checks that the documented annotation is used.
question: Which @JobWorker parameter annotation materializes process variables as a strongly typed object for the handler?

options.text: Use the @CustomHeaders annotation on a MyProcessVariables parameter so the starter binds the class fields to the activated job variables and returns the updated object to Zeebe automatically
Use the @Variable annotation on a MyProcessVariables parameter so the starter binds the class fields to the activated job variables and returns the updated object to Zeebe automatically
Use the @VariableMap annotation on a MyProcessVariables parameter so the starter binds the class fields to the activated job variables and returns the updated object to Zeebe automatically
Use the @VariablesAsType annotation on a MyProcessVariables parameter so the starter binds the class fields to the activated job variables and returns the updated object to Zeebe automatically

a.text: Incorrect. @CustomHeaders binds a Map<String, String> of job custom headers and has no effect on the variable fetching behaviour for the worker.

b.text: Incorrect. @Variable binds a single process variable to a method parameter by name; it does not materialize an entire typed object from the variable map.

c.text: Incorrect. The starter does not document a @VariableMap annotation; the documented bulk-binding annotation for typed variable objects is @VariablesAsType.

d.text: Correct. @VariablesAsType maps the activated process variables into a custom class such as MyProcessVariables, comparable to getVariablesAsType, and Jackson's @JsonProperty annotation is respected for field mapping.

explanation: The starter exposes the @VariablesAsType annotation to map activated process variables into a custom class. The names of the fields of the type join the list of variables to fetch, and returning the object pushes any changes back to Zeebe on auto-completion.
